payload.text:
quiero que me hagas una invitación virtual  para una boda quiero que sea una  invitación muy dinámica y de nivel experto profesional al iniciar quiero que solo haya una ventana abrir invitación, que tenga confirmación de asistencia también con un contador de la fecha, y la fecha que este resaltado en vista de calendario también quiero que vaya un una vista del localización o ubicación, también créame la parte del programa - algo asi; Itinerario de boda 
5:00-5:45 P. M.
Ceremonia
5:00-7:00 Р. М.
Hora de cócteles
7:00-7:15 Р. М.
Los invitados se sientan para cenar
7:20-7:25 P. M.
Intro a la fiesta nupcial
7:25-7:30 P. M.
Primer baile de los recién casados
7:30-7:50 P. M.
Brindis de bienvenida por el padre de la novia
:50-8:15 P. М.
Se sirve la entrada
3:15-9:00 Р. М.
Primer plato y brindis
9:00-9:45 P. M.
Baile de padre e hija.
Nombres de los Novios: Alejandro Castañeda y Lucía Fernandez
al abrir la invitacion que se reprodusca automaticamente la Musica 
nombre de los padrinso quiero que te inventes, 
el color del diseo que sea muy formal utiliza de referencia el color de las imagen que te mando los enlaces.
Foto principal (para la sección donde aparecen los nombres Miguel Ángel & Gabriela Mayte): 

https://res.cloudinary.com/dcnynnstm/image/upload/v1767900578/06_xyon3q.jpg 

  

presentacion de fotos de los novios: 

-https://res.cloudinary.com/dcnynnstm/image/upload/v1767900578/02_nvhlgx.jpg 

- https://res.cloudinary.com/dcnynnstm/image/upload/v1767900579/08_pfh9ht.jpg 

- https://res.cloudinary.com/dcnynnstm/image/upload/v1767900579/09_olq7ud.jpg 

- https://res.cloudinary.com/dcnynnstm/image/upload/v1767900578/05_tozjlu.jpg 

- https://res.cloudinary.com/dcnynnstm/image/upload/v1767900578/07_khkp5n.jpg 

La foto principal debe mostrarse en forma elegante, centrada y con bordes suaves. 

Aqui el enlace de la musica: https://res.cloudinary.com/dcnynnstm/video/upload/v1770267002/Alex_Warren_-_Ordinary_Official_Music_Video_kdtsaz.mp3 

Ubicación: https://maps.app.goo.gl/qsUR3tf6GRittyLeA

**Designing the Invitation**

I'm working on the initial concept for a high-end virtual wedding invitation. I've taken note of the names, Alejandro Castañeda & Lucía Fernandez, and the provided music link to incorporate into the design.


**Developing the Interface**

I've moved on to fleshing out the interface. I plan to autoplay the music when the invitation is opened and have incorporated the main photo and image gallery. I also need to invent some names for the godparents. I have decided to incorporate a countdown timer, a calendar view with the date highlighted, and the location section. I am building the framework with an `index.html` file, leveraging Tailwind CDN and Google Fonts for elegant typography, with entry point being `index.tsx`, and overall logic in `App.tsx`.


**Refining the Component Structure**

I've finalized the component structure now. I'm building with these components: Hero, Calendar, Gallery, Itinerary, Location, RSVP, AudioPlayer, and InvitationEnvelope. I'm prioritizing the "Opening Experience" with a wax seal effect or elegant button, and I've selected the color palette, typography and animations, all fitting the design. I also considered the user interaction needed to trigger the music via the "Open Invitation" button, and chose the date December 14, 2025.

entries.description: App metadata and configuration including audio permissions.
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and Google Fonts.
Global constants for the wedding details.
Main application component with envelope logic and scrollable invitation content.

diagnostics.message: Argument of type 'unknown' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.

payload.text:
**Analyzing the Error** Okay, I'm focusing on that type mismatch in `App.tsx` on line 29. The error, "Argument of type 'unknown' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'," is the key issue. I'm carefully examining the surrounding code to understand where this 'unknown' value originates and why it's expected to be a number. This demands an examination of data types and their compatibility within the code. **Clarifying the Source** I'm now carefully re-examining the code snippet, specifically focusing on how the `value` variable is typed within the `map` function. My initial assumption was that `value` would be inferred as a number due to the structure of `timeLeft`. I'm starting to suspect that the typing might be more permissive than anticipated, potentially leading to the `unknown` type, especially when using `Object.entries`. This requires a closer look at the `timeLeft` object's definition and how TypeScript interprets the iteration process. **Pinpointing the Root Cause** I've got it, the issue lies directly with `Object.entries(timeLeft)`. While I expected the TypeScript inference to recognize `value` as a `number` based on the `TimeRemaining` interface, it's defaulting to `unknown`. The root cause is the `Object.entries` function's return type in this context, which is inferring a union type or possibly `unknown` for the value. To resolve this, I now need to explicitly cast `value` to `number` within the map function or, if it allows, refine the return type of `Object.entries` to guarantee the proper inference. **Confirming the Casting** Okay, I'm going to cast `value` as a `number` inside `Math.max(0, value as number)`. I'm confident this solves the type mismatch I'm seeing. I'm also confirming the intended line number, and that the previous line numbers were accurate. **Refining the Solution** I'm now going to focus on where `value` is being used, and consider whether I could just use a ternary, or if `Math.max` is the most appropriate. I will look at that section to see if I can refine the logic to handle the initial calculation, to be sure that the value won't be unknown. **Verifying the Line Numbers** Okay, I'm now verifying the line numbers and ensuring I have the `Math.max` function on line 28, not 29. I am confirming that the reported line numbers are accurate. I'm focusing on why the `value` is being interpreted as `unknown` specifically within that function call. I need to make sure my count is accurate to provide an accurate assessment. **Reassessing the Error** I am now carefully re-examining the potential error line. It is not within the ternary, which only handles strings. The issue must be within the `Math.max` function, and I'm positive that it is on line 28, not 29. My TypeScript version, or its configuration, is treating `Object.entries(timeLeft)` as returning `[string, unknown][]`. This makes `value` unknown. I'm going to recount again to be sure I have the accurate line number. **Confirming the Scope** I'm now zeroing in on the specific code block. The problem, as confirmed, is on line 28, within the `Math.max` function call. I need to be certain that the data passed to it is what I think it should be. The goal is to determine the scope of this particular issue. ``` 0, minutes: 0, seconds: 0 });` Line 8: `` Line 9: ` useEffect(() => {` Line 10: ` const timer = setInterval(() => {` Line 11: ` const now = new Date().getTime();` Line 12: ` const distance = WEDDING_DATE.getTime() - now;` Line 13: `` Line 14: ` setTimeLeft({` Line 15: ` days: Math.floor(distance / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24)),` 16: ` hours: Math.floor((distance % (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24)) / (1000 * 60 * 60)),` 17: ` minutes: Math.floor((distance % (1000 * 60 * 60)) / (1000 * 60)),` 18: ` seconds: Math.floor((distance % (1000 * 60)) / 1000),` 19: ` });` 20: ` }, 1000);` 21: ` return () => clearInterval(timer);` 22: ` }, []);` 23: `` 24: ` return (` 25: `
` 26: ` {Object.entries(timeLeft).map(([unit, value]) => (` 27: `
` 28: ` {Math.max(0, value)}` ``` **Confirming the Casting** Okay, the issue is at the `Math.max` call on line 28. It now demands that I cast `value` as a `number` to satisfy the TypeScript compiler. The surrounding code confirms that this is the best, easiest fix. I'm focusing specifically on line 28 to ensure the fix is applied accurately. **Verifying the Solution** Okay, the issue is at the `Math.max` call on line 28. It's clear that I need to cast `value` to a number, as the context now shows that it will default to unknown. The surrounding code is exactly what I thought it was. I'm focusing on that precise line to implement the correct type assertion. ``` 0, minutes: 0, seconds: 0 });` Line 8: `` Line 9: ` useEffect(() => {` Line 10: ` const timer = setInterval(() => {` Line 11: ` const now = new Date().getTime();` Line 12: ` const distance = WEDDING_DATE.getTime() - now;` Line 13: `` Line 14: ` setTimeLeft({` Line 15: ` days: Math.floor(distance / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24)),` 16: ` hours: Math.floor((distance % (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24)) / (1000 * 60 * 60)),` 17: ` minutes: Math.floor((distance % (1000 * 60 * 60)) / (1000 * 60)),` 18: ` seconds: Math.floor((distance % (1000 * 60)) / 1000),` 19: ` });` 20: ` }, 1000);` 21: ` return () => clearInterval(timer);` 22: ` }, []);` 23: `` 24: ` return (` 25: `
` 26: ` {Object.entries(timeLeft).map(([unit, value]) => (` 27: `
` 28: ` {Math.max(0, value)}` ``` **Refining the Calendar** I'm now diving into the calendar component code. I need to handle the month's days and offsets. It must display December 2025. I also need to display the start offset for the first day. I'm ensuring everything is accurate. ``` (null); const toggleModal = () => setIsOpen(!isOpen); useEffect(() => { if (audioRef.current) { audioRef.current.volume = 0.5; // Optional: Adjust volume } }, []); return (
{isOpen && (
)}
); }; export default App; ``` **Examining the Component** Now I'm focusing on the calendar component, specifically the December 2025 display. I'm going to determine how to compute the number of days for the month and calculate the correct offset. I'm focusing on the component's internal logic. I'm now determining how to handle the start offset for the first day of the month. I'm carefully reviewing all calculations. **Refining the Calendar** Okay, I'm thinking about the logic within the calendar. I am now trying to accurately count the days of the month, and I'm figuring out how to calculate the correct offset for the first day, so it correctly displays December 2025. I am verifying the calculations and ensure the logic is precise. **Calculating the Offsets** I'm now implementing the calculation of the correct first day offset and the number of days in December 2025 within the calendar component. I am carefully ensuring the logic is accurate for proper rendering. \n\n I'm now refining how the calendar renders the days and offsets. I'm focusing on December 2025 and making sure the correct number of days are displayed. **Refining the Day Display** I am now focused on the rendering of December 2025 within the calendar. I am carefully implementing the logic to calculate and display the days correctly, and ensuring the display offset is correct. **Reviewing the Gallery** I'm now shifting gears to the gallery section, focusing on its visual structure and image loading. I'll inspect the responsiveness. I'm checking each image's properties, making certain they load and render correctly. \n\n I've checked the image responsiveness, and they appear to load correctly. Now, I'm verifying the images are being pulled from the `IMAGES.gallery` array and are mapped correctly. **Verifying the Gallery** I'm now making certain the gallery's visual structure is good, and am checking image loading. The responsiveness checks out. I'm verifying the data source for the images and its proper mapping in the code. I am confirming the `item` property of each image is correct. **Displaying the Offsets** Now, I'm focusing on accurately calculating and rendering December 2025 within the calendar component. I'm carefully implementing the logic to correctly display the correct number of days, and their offset. I'm making sure it works. **Refining the RSVP Form** I'm now diving into the RSVP form. I need to handle input validation, and make sure the submit button works to send the user's information. I'm focusing on user input, and confirming that the form handles submissions correctly. **Validating the Form** I am now meticulously examining the RSVP form's structure. I'm focusing on input validation to ensure all required fields are filled and formatted correctly, and that submission triggers an action. I need to make certain that the code is set up properly.

entries.description: Fix TypeScript error where Object.entries value is inferred as unknown in Math.max on line 28 (noted as 29 in the error report)

payload.text: la es 14 de diciembre del 2026 y la galería que sea animada como presentación magica, al abrir la invitacion se reprodusca la musica automaticamente, y ponme animaciones de entarada y salida de todo

**Revising the Date**

I'm focused on the wedding invitation application and the immediate need to update the date. My primary task is to change the displayed date from December 14, 2025, to December 14, 2026. This seems straightforward, but I'm thinking about where this date is stored and how it's used in the application.


**Developing the Visuals**

I'm now integrating animations and a formal aesthetic into the wedding invitation. I'm prioritizing the slideshow for the gallery using either cross-fading or a smooth slide effect. I'll be enhancing the visual style, including fading sections upon scroll and ensuring the color palette matches the formal gold/beige/black theme. The date update, for December 14, 2026, is confirmed in `constants.tsx` and the calendar component.

Para elevar la invitación al nivel de "experto profesional" y cumplir con tus nuevas peticiones, realizaré los siguientes cambios:

1.  **Actualización de Fecha**: Cambiaremos todo a **14 de diciembre de 2026**.
2.  **Calendario 2026**: Ajustaremos el offset del calendario para que el 1 de diciembre de 2026 (que es martes) se muestre correctamente.
3.  **Galería "Mágica"**: Implementaremos un carrusel animado con transiciones de desvanecimiento (fade) para una presentación más elegante y profesional.
4.  **Animaciones de Entrada**: Utilizaremos el `Intersection Observer API` para crear un efecto de revelado (fade-in up) conforme el usuario hace scroll hacia abajo, dando una sensación de fluidez y dinamismo.
5.  **Refuerzo de Audio**: Aseguraremos que la música inicie inmediatamente al presionar el botón de "Abrir Invitación".

Aquí tienes el código actualizado:

entries.description: Actualización de la fecha de la boda a 2026.
Implementación de galería mágica animada, calendario 2026, y animaciones de scroll profesionales.

payload.text: Cambiame el diseño del contador que tenga una animacion de ojas de calendario osea que cada S/M/H/D que pase sea una oja volteandoce del calendario que se un estilo boda y que vea en 3D

**Designing the Leaf Flip**

I'm now focused on the wedding invitation countdown timer redesign, specifically the "calendar leaf flipping" animation. The core challenge is making a visually appealing leaf-flipping style animation for the seconds. I'm exploring how to best achieve this aesthetic detail.

entries.description: Add 3D Flip animation styles for the calendar-leaf countdown.
Update Countdown component with 3D calendar leaf flipping animation.

diagnostics.message: Type 'number' has no properties in common with type 'Properties<string | number, string & {}>'.
The left-hand side of an arithmetic operation must be of type 'any', 'number', 'bigint' or an enum type.
No value exists in scope for the shorthand property 'z'. Either declare one or provide an initializer.

payload.text:
**Addressing the Errors**

I'm currently focused on rectifying the type mismatch error on line 81 of `App.tsx`. The core issue appears to stem from an incompatible type assignment involving a number and a more complex property type. I am investigating the specific variable and its intended usage within the `Properties` context. Additionally, I need to resolve the arithmetic operation type issue, also on line 81. I'm exploring the expected data types and how the operation is being performed.


**Correcting Styles and Scope**

I've made progress by correcting the `z-index` property in the inline style attribute to `zIndex`. This addresses several errors on line 81 related to type mismatches and undeclared variables. The problem was the invalid syntax in the inline style, causing the engine to misinterpret `z-index` as an arithmetic expression. This also clarifies the earlier "cannot find name 'index'" error, which was a consequence of the incorrect parsing. I'm now looking at why `div` is undefined.


**Analyzing the Logic**

I've realized that the issue with `z-index` in the inline style was indeed a syntax error. TS was interpreting `z-index` as a subtraction. The correct syntax is `zIndex`. My previous fixes resolved this, but then revealed a new error: the code could not find the `div` identifier. This is on line 88. Now, I'm carefully counting lines in the file to determine the true context. I had a bug and was reading the wrong context to line 88. I'm carefully double checking the code I've been given to compare it to the context. I'll need to assess why it can't find the closing div.


**Verifying the Context**

I'm confident that addressing the `z-index` syntax on line 81 as `zIndex` will resolve a cascading issue, hopefully clarifying the "Cannot find name 'div'" error on line 88. My previous efforts should resolve the conflict, since line 81 needs to be corrected and I can begin looking forward. If my changes fix the syntax error, I'll move on to a new issue.


**Reviewing the Scope**

I've decided to expand my review beyond the initially identified lines. After re-reading the error messages and the `App.tsx` file (which appears to be a wedding invitation), I am now addressing the root cause: the erroneous use of `z-index` in the inline style, which is causing misinterpretation as an arithmetic expression. I'll need to focus on correct the syntax to `zIndex` and revisit the whole file to confirm other issues. I'll avoid changing the file structure or using deprecated APIs as requested.


**Confirming the Errors**

I've confirmed that the 'Cannot find name `index`' error arises because the parser misinterprets the invalid object property on line 81. I am fixing the `z-index` syntax and reviewing the code. I am confident that the code will function correctly after the fix. It looks like the rest of the file is working, including usages of `IMAGES.music` and the gallery array. The date is correct. I am ready to close this task, assuming the errors are gone after line 81 is corrected.


**Validating the Dates**

I've validated the date calculations and the start offset to confirm that December 1st, 2026, is indeed a Tuesday, matching the comment. I've also verified that the form and select element are likely correct based on their general use cases, including the missing `defaultValue` or `value`. I believe the remaining errors are resolved.



**Implementing the Fix**

I've implemented the fix for line 81 by changing `z-index` to `zIndex` in the inline style of the flip card bottom div, and I'm ready to confirm that this resolves the parsing errors. The change is simple, but the cascading effects it was causing were significant. I'm now waiting to confirm that there are no further parsing issues.


**Confirming the Update**

I've reviewed the provided code snippet, focusing on the `FlipUnit` component and its logic. I've noted the use of `useEffect` to manage the flipping animation between values. I'm ready to integrate the changes into the broader context, ensuring compatibility with the existing application structure. I'll focus on making sure I can integrate the new file without errors.


**Analyzing the New Code**

I'm now diving into the latest code snippet, specifically the `FlipUnit` component, to ensure the changes I've made integrate correctly. I'm focusing on the `formattedNext` variable and the structure of the `flip-container`. I'll need to check how the flipping animation is handled, including the `isFlipping` state.


**Integrating the Changes**

I'm now integrating the new `FlipUnit` component into the existing code base, carefully observing the `useEffect` hook and state management of `isFlipping`. I've made sure to keep the changes isolated, and I'll confirm that there are no conflicts or unexpected behavior. I'm especially focused on how the animation interacts with the component's internal state.


**Verifying the Gallery**

I'm now carefully examining the image gallery component, focusing on the mapping of images and the conditional styling of the navigation buttons. I want to confirm that the `currentIndex` state variable correctly reflects the currently displayed image and that the button styles update accordingly. I'll need to check the event handler for button clicks. I'm especially focused on the transition between images.


**Evaluating the Calendar**

I'm now carefully examining the calendar component and its logic. I'm focusing on the date calculations, including the use of `Array.from` to generate the days, and the `startOffset` variable. I want to confirm that the displayed calendar correctly represents December 2026, considering the specified starting day. I'll need to double-check the accuracy of the date generation logic and the offset.

I'm now carefully checking the calendar's day generation, particularly the `days` array and the `startOffset`, ensuring the week displays and starts correctly for December 2026. The structure of the calendar component appears straightforward. I need to ensure there are no off-by-one errors with the date calculations or offset implementation. I'm now carefully examining the opening section, specifically the conditional rendering and the animation. The handling of the `isOpen` state and the associated UI elements seems clear, and I'm ensuring the transitions and animations are smooth. I'm taking another pass on the `handleOpen` click handler to ensure proper state management and navigation within the component.

I'm now carefully examining the button component in the opening section. I am focused on the `handleOpen` click handler's implementation and how it transitions the user's view. **Verifying the Links**

I'm now carefully examining the location section and verifying the Google Maps link to ensure it's functional and leads to the correct destination. I'm checking the formatting, specifically the inline styling, and the positioning of the "UBICACIÓN" text, and making sure the text is accurate. entries.description: Fix CSS property name 'z-index' to 'zIndex' in the style object of the flip card bottom div to resolve syntax and parsing errors.
